Antilag Boost Target.short: Boost target used while anti-lag is active.
Antilag Boost Target.long: This table sets the boost level the tune tries to maintain during anti-lag. It works together with fuel, spark retard, and timeout safeguards to trade spool speed against heat and stress. Conservative values are usually the safer starting point.

Antilag Coolant Safety (Maximum).short: Safety or enable setting for anti-lag operation.
Antilag Coolant Safety (Maximum).long: This family handles anti-lag activation, delays, timers, or safety gates such as coolant and EGT limits. WGDC Base.short: Wastegate duty control table used by the boost controller.
WGDC Base.long: This family is part of the closed-loop WGDC system and shapes how aggressively boost is built and held. It works together with boost ceilings and commanded WGDC to determine boost behavior. Logs are mandatory when changing these.

WGDC Base (Custom).short: Wastegate duty control table used by the boost controller.

WGDC Base (Custom) (Antilag).short: Safety or enable setting for anti-lag operation.
WGDC Base (Custom) (Antilag).long: This family handles anti-lag activation, delays, timers, or safety gates such as coolant and EGT limits. These settings are mainly there to control when anti-lag is allowed and how long it can run. They are important for keeping a spool feature from turning into a hardware-killing feature.

WGDC D-Factor.short: Derivative gain for wastegate duty correction.
WGDC D-Factor.long: This table damps boost control by reacting to how quickly error is changing. It helps control overshoot and settle the turbo after a fast spool event. Too much D can make the system noisy or inconsistent.

WGDC PID Adder Ceiling.short: Bound on how much the WGDC controller is allowed to add or subtract.
WGDC PID Adder Ceiling.long: These tables cap the wastegate controller's correction range so the closed-loop system cannot run away. They are important when the tune uses aggressive PID or large base duty values. Keep enough room for correction without letting the controller abuse the hardware.

Wastegate Base Finalizer (Bank 1).short: Final shaping table applied to base wastegate duty.
Wastegate Base Finalizer (Bank 1).long: This looks like a last-stage modifier to the wastegate base calculation, likely split by bank on twin-path hardware definitions. It is a companion table used to refine the commanded duty before or after main feedforward logic. Change carefully and verify bank-to-bank consistency.

Wastegate Position.short: Wastegate position reference or actuator-related helper table.

Wastegate opening for sound codeword.short: Feature, patch, or diagnostic enable/disable flag.
Wastegate opening for sound codeword.long: These are utility-style switches that turn diagnostics, patch features, or behavior gates on or off. They are not power tables by themselves, but they can change what other tables are allowed to do. Use them carefully and only when you understand the side effects.
